text: Git branching is an essential part of version control that helps teams work together efficiently. A common workflow involves creating a feature branch from the main branch. Each developer can work on their own feature branch without affecting the stable code. Once the work is complete, the developer creates a pull request. This allows other team members to review the changes, discuss improvements, and catch errors before they are merged. After approval, the feature branch is merged into the main branch. This structured process keeps the project organized and ensures that only well-tested code becomes part of the final product.
